title: Vernonanthura oligolepis
taxonomy: Vernonanthura oligolepis is classified within the family Asteraceae and the genus Vernonanthura, which is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The genus Vernonanthura is a member of the tribe Vernonieae. The plant belongs to the phylum Magnoliophyta, which is a division of the kingdom Plantae. Vernonanthura oligolepis also falls under the broader category of eudicots, a subcategory of angiosperms. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of its taxonomic group.

intro: Vernonanthura oligolepis, also known as Brazilian pennyroyal, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Brazil. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Vernonanthura oligolepis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and respiratory infections.
